perturbation.original_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NCB shall ultimately be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract, despite NPC's operational role.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that NPC is responsible for all credit, fraud, and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract. The modified text states that NCB is ultimately responsible for these risks, creating a direct contradiction regarding risk allocation. This creates uncertainty about which party bears the financial responsibility for merchant-related losses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NCB shall ultimately be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract, despite NPC's operational role.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.3 states that 'NCB shall ultimately be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract', while the original text stated that 'NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract'. This creates a direct contradiction because the responsibility for credit, fraud, and other risks is assigned to different parties in the original and modified versions, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes about financial liability, which aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 INDEMNIFICATION. NPC agrees to indemnify, defend and save NCB, its directors, officers and employees harmless from all losses, claims, judgments, awards, penalties, expenses and other amounts of any nature arising out of: (a) NPC's failure to perform this Agreement in accordance with its terms, including, but not limited to, the failure to pay expenses, charges and other amounts in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, 4 5 (b) The negligent exercise of or the exceeding by any Joint Officer of the authority granted pursuant to this Agreement to act as an officer of NCB, or, (c) NCB's grant of authority to NPC pursuant to this Agreement, including, but not limited to, all court costs, investigation expenses and the reasonable fees and expenses of separate counsel for NCB selected by NCB, provided, however, that NCB shall not be entitled to indemnification as to amounts arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of NCB.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 INDEMNIFICATION. NPC agrees to indemnify, defend and save NCB, its directors, officers and employees harmless from all losses, claims, judgments, awards, penalties, expenses and other amounts of any nature arising out of: (a) NPC's failure to perform this Agreement in accordance with its terms, including, but not limited to, the failure to pay expenses, charges and other amounts in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, (b) The negligent exercise of or the exceeding by any Joint Officer of the authority granted pursuant to this Agreement to act as an officer of NCB, or, (c) NCB's grant of authority to NPC pursuant to this Agreement, including, but not limited to, all court costs, investigation expenses and the reasonable fees and expenses of separate counsel for NCB selected by NCB, regardless of whether the losses are caused by the negligence or willful misconduct of NCB.
perturbation.explanation: The original indemnification clause states that NCB is not entitled to indemnification for amounts arising from its own negligence or willful misconduct. The modified clause removes this exception, requiring NPC to indemnify NCB even for NCB's own negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the original provision, creating uncertainty about the scope of NPC's indemnification obligations. It's a complete reversal of the original intent, potentially exposing NPC to much greater liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 INDEMNIFICATION. NPC agrees to indemnify, defend and save NCB, its directors, officers and employees harmless from all losses, claims, judgments, awards, penalties, expenses and other amounts of any nature arising out of: (a) NPC's failure to perform this Agreement in accordance with its terms, including, but not limited to, the failure to pay expenses, charges and other amounts in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, (b) The negligent exercise of or the exceeding by any Joint Officer of the authority granted pursuant to this Agreement to act as an officer of NCB, or, (c) NCB's grant of authority to NPC pursuant to this Agreement, including, but not limited to, all court costs, investigation expenses and the reasonable fees and expenses of separate counsel for NCB selected by NCB, regardless of whether the losses are caused by the negligence or willful misconduct of NCB.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that NCB is not entitled to indemnification for amounts arising from its own negligence or willful misconduct. The changed text removes this exception and states that NPC will indemnify NCB regardless of whether the losses are caused by NCB's negligence or willful misconduct. This directly contradicts the original provision, creating a clear and substantial conflict within the document. The contradicted text section is now stating the inverse of the original clause that limited NPC's obligations in cases of NCB negligence.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall be five years commencing on the Effective Date and ending at the close of business on the fifth anniversary of the Effective Date. This Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless one party gives the other party written notice of non-renewal at least six months prior to automatic renewal.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall be five years commencing on the Effective Date and ending at the close of business on the fifth anniversary of the Effective Date. This Agreement will not renew automatically. The agreement will be reassessed by both parties upon conclusion of the initial term, with renewal subject to mutual consent, under conditions defined at the time.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies automatic renewal unless a party provides written notice of non-renewal six months prior. The modified clause states the agreement will not automatically renew, and renewal is subject to mutual consent, explicitly contradicting the auto-renewal term of the initial agreement. This creates uncertainty about renewal, which is a fundamental term. This affects the obligations and expectations for both parties after the initial five-year term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall be five years commencing on the Effective Date and ending at the close of business on the fifth anniversary of the Effective Date. This Agreement will not renew automatically. The agreement will be reassessed by both parties upon conclusion of the initial term, with renewal subject to mutual consent, under conditions defined at the time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement automatically renews. The changed text explicitly states the agreement will not automatically renew and requires mutual consent for renewal. This directly contradicts the automatic renewal clause and therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.
